security_gap.gap_description: Vote input may be spoofed without signature verification.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system accepts unsigned votes from external actors.

assumptions: The governance contract does not enforce signature verification for vote messages.
The actor is able to access the voting interface without authentication.

mitigation.strategy: Require signature-based vote authentication and role validation.

description: An attacker may impersonate a user and submit fraudulent votes to the governance contract.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes may be manipulated without sufficient integrity checks.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system lacks tamper-proof mechanisms for vote submission.

assumptions: The governance contract does not verify the authenticity of vote messages.
The actor can alter the vote data before submission.

mitigation.strategy: Implement mechanisms to prevent vote tampering, such as using immutable data structures or cryptographic hashing.

description: An attacker may modify the content of a vote before it is recorded on the blockchain.

security_gap.gap_description: The system lacks mechanisms to ensure non-repudiation of votes.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system does not provide mechanisms to prove the origin of votes.

assumptions: The voting system does not employ techniques to prevent repudiation of votes.
The actor can deny having cast a specific vote.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cryptographic techniques to ensure non-repudiation of votes, such as digital signatures or blockchain-based auditing.

description: An attacker may cast a vote and later deny having done so.

security_gap.gap_description: Votes may be disclosed to unauthorized parties.

actor_risk.description: The actor is unauthenticated and can perform governance actions.

behavioral_risk.rationale: The system may not adequately protect vote privacy.

assumptions: The governance contract does not protect vote confidentiality.
The actor can access the voting data through a vulnerability in the system.

mitigation.strategy: Use privacy-enhancing technologies, such as zero-knowledge proofs or homomorphic encryption, to protect the confidentiality of votes.

description: An attacker might be able to obtain information about the votes cast.
